name: dashboard-shell
title: Dashboard Shell
description: App frame for clinical dashboards: an optional fixed sidebar, a sticky header, and a centered scrolling content region. Ships DashboardHeader, the standard bar every block's header slot uses.

props.name: sidebar
props.description: Left nav column. Static when the shell is wide; a toggled drawer (with a header hamburger) when narrow.

props.name: header
props.description: Sticky top bar. Use DashboardHeader unless you need something bespoke.

props.name: collapsible
props.description: Enable the narrow-width drawer + header toggle.

props.name: DashboardHeader: icon / title / subtitle / actions / tone
props.description: The standard header bar: the icon is sized and coloured here, the title truncates, and the actions wrap below it rather than overflowing. `tone="destructive"` for surfaces whose subject is urgency.

clinicalNotes: Sized by CONTAINER queries, not the viewport, so the shell adapts to the width it's embedded in — a split pane, a modal, or the docs preview — not just the browser size. That promise is only kept if nothing inside it reaches for a viewport breakpoint, which is why the content padding and DashboardHeader both step at `@2xl/shell` rather than `md:`: a block in a narrow column must not get desktop padding merely because the browser window is wide. When the container is wide the sidebar is static; when narrow it collapses to a toggled drawer with a hamburger in the header, so navigation is never lost on a bedside tablet. The header is sticky so the patient banner (name, MRN, allergies) stays on screen while a clinician scrolls a long chart — a core patient-safety pattern.
notes: Layer-M0 scaffolding. Client component. The container-query outer frame of every block. v0.2.0 adds DashboardHeader and moves the content padding off viewport breakpoints.
